explanations.1: Nociceptors are the specific type of receptor cell that transduces painful stimuli, whereas mechanoreceptors respond to physical pressure or touch.
explanations.2: The facial nerve (cranial nerve VII) is one of the nerves responsible for conveying taste (gustatory) information to the brain.
explanations.3: The sour taste submodality is a response to the concentration of hydrogen ions (H+), which is what defines the pH of a solution like saliva.
explanations.4: While photoreceptors and bipolar cells process light within the retina, it is the axons of the retinal ganglion cells that bundle together to form the optic nerve.
explanations.5: The hair cells in the inner ear are mechanoreceptors because they are physically moved by sound waves or head position changes to produce a signal.
explanations.6: Auditory information (audition) passes through the medial geniculate nucleus of the thalamus, while the ventral posterior nucleus handles somatosensory and gustatory inputs.
explanations.7: The cochlear nucleus in the medulla receives auditory signals from the ear and projects them to the inferior colliculus in the midbrain for processing.
explanations.8: Visual processing is both inverted and reversed; therefore, stimuli from the upper-left visual field are processed in the inferior-right region of the primary visual cortex.
explanations.9: The sensory homunculus shows that highly sensitive areas with more receptors, like the lips and hands, have a disproportionately large representation in the somatosensory cortex.
explanations.10: Axons from the vestibular ganglion project to the vestibular nuclei in the brainstem, and some also project directly to the cerebellum to assist with balance.
explanations.11: The primary motor cortex in the precentral gyrus is the region that provides the final output to motor neurons in the brainstem and spinal cord to initiate movement.
explanations.12: The vestibulospinal tract is an extrapyramidal pathway that uses equilibrium information from the vestibular system to modulate posture and balance.
explanations.13: The ventral horn of the spinal cord contains the cell bodies of motor neurons that send their axons out to innervate and contract skeletal muscles.
explanations.14: The withdrawal reflex is a somatic reflex that triggers the contraction of muscles to pull a limb away from a painful or harmful stimulus.
explanations.15: The homunculus is the term for the topographical map that represents the sensory input from various body parts onto the somatosensory cortex.
